title: Diving to Earth's Deepest Point
story: Plunging into the inky darkness, filmmaker James Cameron made history on March 26, 2012. He became the first person in over 50 years to reach Challenger Deep, the deepest known spot on Earth. Cameron's specially designed submarine withstood crushing pressure as it descended nearly seven miles below the ocean's surface. This daring expedition opened new doors for deep-sea exploration and scientific discovery.
activity_question: What did James Cameron become the first person to do in over 50 years?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to explore the deepest parts of our oceans?

story: Bats were ready, but politics stepped up to the plate. A historic baseball series between the Baltimore Orioles and Cincinnati Reds was set for Havana, Cuba in 1960. But on March 26, plans suddenly changed. Rising tensions between the U.S. and Cuba forced the games to relocate to Miami, Florida. This last-minute switch marked a turning point in sports and international relations.
activity_question: Where was the baseball series originally planned to take place?

title: Breaking Barriers in the Courtroom
story: History was made when William H. Hastie took his seat as a judge. In 1937, he became the first Black person to serve as a federal judge in the United States. Hastie's appointment to the court in the Virgin Islands opened doors for future generations. His groundbreaking role on March 26 marked a significant step toward equality in the legal system.
activity_question: What position did William H. Hastie achieve in 1937?
activity_choices: First Black lawyer in the U.S.
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for judges to come from diverse backgrounds?

title: Breaking Barriers at the Stock Exchange
story: History was made in 1973 when Susan Shaw shattered a centuries-old tradition. She became the first woman to step onto the trading floor of London's Stock Exchange since it opened in 1802. On March 26, Shaw's presence marked a significant milestone for gender equality in the world of finance.
activity_question: What did Susan Shaw become the first woman to do?
activity_choices: Step onto the trading floor of London's Stock Exchange
Become the CEO of a major bank
Write a book about stock market investing
activity_answer: Step onto the trading floor of London's Stock Exchange
follow_up_question: Why do you think it took so long for a woman to be allowed on the trading floor?
